refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined the entire load→travel→dump cycle into a single requirement, which includes multiple distinct phases. To be atomic and solution-neutral, the FR is split into sub-FRs each addressing one phase of the cycle. This refinement focuses on the load phase as one atomic functional requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load phase under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, ensuring the p95 cycle time is less than or equal to the customer-defined target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load mechanism speed and efficiency
Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback
Design load phase process to minimize idle and transition times
Allocate sufficient power and torque resources to meet cycle time targets
Integrate synchronized coordination between load and travel phases
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design load phase process to minimize idle and transition times

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on minimizing idle and transition times within the load phase itself, which is logically independent from travel and dump phases, supporting decoupled design.

assumptions_risks: Cycle time target is achievable given physical and operational constraints
Real-time feedback or adaptive control is not mandatory at this level
Load phase can be optimized independently without causing negative impacts on travel or dump phases
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including coordination mechanisms with travel and dump phases in subsequent FRs to ensure overall cycle time optimization and avoid hidden coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system logic and coordination processes, including load phase process design to optimize operational efficiency.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends operational status and performance data during load phase.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives and manages task assignments related to load phase activities.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication with external modules and systems during load phase.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as load phase process is a core system coordination function; minimal diffusion as most related data and control remain internal.
Vehicle module handles physical operations but load phase process involves broader coordination beyond vehicle control, causing diffusion.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control; load phase optimization is broader and not limited to teleoperation commands.
Safety Services focus on safety alerts and monitoring; load phase process is operational and not safety-specific, leading to poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the load phase process DP because it centralizes system coordination and process optimization, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if physical constraints impact load phase timing.
Dependency on external interfaces may cause some diffusion outside NGHE Solution.
Changes in tasking or telemetry interfaces could require adjustments in module boundaries.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment issues arise due to physical vehicle constraints, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate coordination logic (in NGHE Solution) from vehicle-specific timing adjustments (in Vehicle module) to improve modular clarity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Design load phase process to minimize idle and transition times' directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase. The DP logically focuses on optimizing the load phase timing, which is the core functional requirement.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if physical constraints impact load phase timing.
Dependency on external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) may cause some diffusion outside NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion for the load phase process DP, centralizing system coordination and process optimization. Other modules like Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services have lower containment scores and higher diffusion, indicating that NGHE Solution is the best containment boundary currently.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the acceptance criteria specify measurable cycle time performance (p95 ≤ CT_target) under defined duty cycle and site conditions, which can be monitored via the Telemetry interface.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the load phase process DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear, with well-defined external interfaces supporting operational data exchange and task management relevant to the load phase.

recommended_revisions: Consider refining the DP to explicitly separate coordination logic (in NGHE Solution) from vehicle-specific timing adjustments (in Vehicle module) if physical vehicle constraints significantly impact load phase timing, to reduce diffusion risk and improve modular clarity.
Monitor dependencies on external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) to manage potential diffusion and maintain clear module boundaries.
Review and update module boundaries if changes in tasking or telemetry interfaces occur to ensure continued containment and cohesion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear logical mapping, feasible verification, and acceptable containment despite some diffusion risks. The DP directly addresses the FR, and the IM allocation is clear with manageable external dependencies. Given the high safety criticality, further decomposition at this stage is not warranted to avoid unnecessary complexity and maintain focus on verification and risk management. Recommended revisions focus on monitoring and refining module boundaries rather than decomposing further.

revise.why: Consider refining the DP to explicitly separate coordination logic in NGHE Solution from vehicle-specific timing adjustments in Vehicle module to reduce diffusion risk and improve modular clarity.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently captures the load phase timing optimization needed to meet cycle time targets.
External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) will remain stable or changes will be managed through boundary reviews.
open_risks: Potential overlap and diffusion with Vehicle module due to physical constraints impacting load phase timing.
Dependency on external interfaces may cause diffusion outside NGHE Solution module.
evidence_needed: Performance data confirming cycle time targets are met under defined conditions.
Verification results from telemetry monitoring and task management interfaces.
Updates on interface changes and their impact on module boundaries.
